As someone who's curious but not very open-minded—my openness score is only 25—I find it challenging when I see posts that are clearly made up or misleading.\n\nI remember when my son was little, we used to talk about the internet and how important it is for everyone these days. response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
I've been using social media for years now, ever since it became popular in the early days back when I was still working as a carpenter. You know, like Facebook and Instagram. It's fascinating how these platforms have grown, but lately, there seems to be an awful lot of fake content floating around. As someone who's curious but not very open-minded—my openness score is only 25—I find it challenging when I see posts that are clearly made up or misleading.

I remember when my son was little, we used to talk about the internet and how important it is for everyone these days. He went through some vocational training himself, you might say, so he's more tech-savvy than me. But even with all the fake news around, I try to stay informed because being in a relationship means we discuss current events often, especially when planning things like visiting relatives in Bari or maybe checking on how business is doing.

Being somewhat conscientious—just barely, score 6—I guess you could say that my approach to dealing with this fake content involves carefully verifying each piece of information before accepting it as true. It's not easy, though; I spend several hours daily scrolling through social media because I'm still quite active online despite being a bit neurotic and sensitive about things.

The constant barrage of misinformation makes me feel anxious sometimes—my high neuroticism score shows that—and I worry about how it affects our community. But with my moderate agreeableness, I don't want to completely distrust everyone or just ignore the whole thing; there's a middle path here.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Based on my background as an older Italian man curious but somewhat closed off mentally, and given that fake content is becoming increasingly common online while spending significant time on social media platforms like Facebook daily, I find myself often verifying information due to moderate agreeableness preventing extreme skepticism.
response_parsed.tone: Reflective with a touch of anxiety
